query: Glimpse: Fast, local-first, Open-Source dictation built for you complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Whispering: Open-source, local-first dictation you can trust
results.content:
Hey everyone 👋 Braden here, creator of Whispering.  
  
For years, I relied on transcription tools that were \almost\ good, but they were all closed-source black boxes. Even those claiming to be “local” left me wondering where my audio really went. And when one pivoted or shut down, my workflow broke overnight.  
  
So I built Whispering. It’s open-source, local-first, and most importantly, transparent with your data. Connect it to ultra-fast APIs like Groq Cloud, or run it entirely offline via Speaches. There is no middleman. For me, the features were good enough that I left my paid tools behind (I used Superwhisper and Wispr Flow before). [...] Whispering is transparent, extensible, and fully hackable under an MIT license. Chain custom transformations for grammar fixes, translation, or formatting. My favorite part? Hands-free, voice-activated dictation. I use it for several hours a day, from coding to thinking out loud while carrying pizza boxes back from the office. [...] Your data stays yours—always. We give you control over how and where it’s processed. Want the speed of the cloud? Connect directly to providers without a middleman. Prefer full privacy? Run everything offline. You can switch anytime, and you’ll always know where your audio goes and how it’s handled. We hope to add more offline models in the future.

results.title: Stop Using Windows Dictation! Use This Free, Offline AI Tool Instead!
results.content:
# Stop Using Windows Dictation! Use This Free, Offline AI Tool Instead!
## Paul O'Malley
43300 subscribers
126 likes

### Description
4093 views
Posted: 1 Feb 2026
Frustrated with the default Windows voice typing (Win+H)? You aren't alone. It cuts off when you think, struggles with accuracy, and just feels clunky compared to modern tools.

In this video, I walk you through how to install and configure Handy—an incredible, free, open-source application that brings professional-grade AI dictation to Windows. Powered by models like OpenAI’s Whisper and Parakeet V3, Handy runs completely offline on your local machine, ensuring your voice data stays private and your text is accurate. [...] 35 comments
### Transcript:
Welcome to this brief guide on how to 
set up Handy voice dictation on Windows. If you're running on Windows, you probably know the frustration of 
the standard voice dictation mode. While Windows Key plus H is a decent built in 
option, it's often slower than most people need, and it has a tendency to cut off if 
you pause to think for even a second. It just doesn't feel as robust as the 
tools available on other platforms. I've featured Mac based programs 
like Whisper Transcription before. But historically, there hasn't been a great, high performance equivalent for Windows 
users that leverages modern AI models. Well, that ends today. We're going to be looking at a 
fantastic program called Handy. Handy is a free, open source [...] Key Features of Handy:
✅ Privacy Focussed: All transcription happens locally on your GPU/CPU.
✅ No Time Limits: Speak for as long as you need without cut-offs.
✅ High Accuracy: Uses advanced AI models for superior punctuation and jargon recognition.
✅ Cross-Platform: Available for Windows (x64 & ARM), Mac, and Linux.

If you found this guide helpful, please give it a thumbs up and subscribe for more productivity tools and software showcases!

Hashtags
#VoiceDictation #WindowsTips #Productivity #OpenSource #HandyApp #AI #SpeechToText #TechTutorial #OfflinePrivacy #Windows11

results.title: Epicenter launches Whispering, an open-source transcription app | Y Combinator posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content: W. built Whispering after frustration with the lack of data transparency and ergonomics in alternatives, and uses Whispering daily in his coding and walking sessions. Whispering is MIT-licensed, open-format, and local-first. It offers hands-free, voice-activated dictation, and enables you to chain custom AI transformations for grammar fixes, translation, or formatting with any model you want. Whispering is the first step toward Epicenter's broader vision: interoperable tools sharing a single, user-owned memory layer, replacing siloed apps with open, extensible alternatives. [...] Epicenter (YC S25) is building a suite of open-source apps that share context in a single folder of plain text and SQLite. They just launched Whispering—their free, open-source, local-first transcription app—as the first step toward their broader vision. Modern transcription tools, and most productivity software, are closed-source black boxes. Even if they claim to be “local,” you can’t see the code or verify where your data goes. Many lock you into their ecosystem, charge subscriptions for functionality you could run yourself, and can break your workflow when they pivot or shut down. Productivity apps need open-source alternatives that are transparent with your data and match them feature-by-feature, with great UX. Braden W. built Whispering after frustration with the lack of data

results.content: joined us as a Builder in Residence at Mozilla.ai. So for those interested in deploying a more raw/lightweight approach to local speech-to-text (or other multimodal) models, feel free to check out llamafile - which includes whisperfile, a single-file whisper.cpp + cosmopolitan framework-based executable. We're hoping to build some bridges between the two projects as well.  | | | |  |  | cootsnuck 86 days ago  | parent | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Yup, Handy is the one that made me stop looking for local open source alternatives to Wispr Flow. results.title: Glimpse - Voice Journal - Ratings & Reviews - App Store - Apple
results.content:
# Glimpse - Voice Journal

## Ratings & Reviews

 4.8

  out of 5

  344 Ratings

 ### Give it a shot

  JGaul33

  I started using Glimpse bc I struggle to take the time to write, and I struggle with what to write. Glimpse lets you get the thoughts out in real time. Transcription needs some work, but you can review before you save. For $40 I recommend to any man struggling to take that first step.
 ### I actually journal now

  caander162 [...] caander162

  I always wanted to be the person with a ton of handwritten journals but it just takes too long. Voice to text journaling is literally perfect it’s like sending a voice note to your friends but it’s all written down for you to go back to and remember. The voice to text is super accurate but cleans up my filler words and the design is so clean. The ability to have AI recap my journal entries and provide insight is insanely cool. Highly recommend to anyone that’s always wanted to journal but just hasn’t gotten around to finding the time for it. You’ll thank yourself in 10 years!!! Love this app
 ### Best journaling app out there!

  ranoya12 [...] Goodnight, Good Luck

  Every new year I pledge to myself that it’ll start journaling. I give it max a month before my fingers cannot possibly keep up with my mind. Glimpse gave me an outlet to let it out without a worry of phrasing things correctly (getting caught in the unnecessary stressors that I’m oh so prone to) and speaking to a neutral source. Good luck to my therapist, my notes are thorough now.
 ### Journaling on a whole new level

  sydjt

  I love this app! As someone who journals often, using voice entries has completely changed the game. It’s so cool having prompts to reflect on and being able to unpack my thoughts using AI. Also love the summary feature because it helps me remember events from the week.
